entry.title: Variant Condition Records NOT Displayed in the Responder when SAP CPQ is Integrated with VCP
entry.content:
The main subject of this article is the issue of displaying variant condition records in Responder when SAP CPQ integrates with SAP ERP (A)VC through SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (VCP for short).

According to Pricing | SAP Help Portal, pricing records of variant condition keys should be listed one by one in the Responder of the Configurator page.

However, some users may find it that even the integration has been set up following the guidance in SAP Help Portal, pricing records of variant condition keys are not displayed in the Responder. Why is this?

Through communication with SAP Tickets, we learned the design idea of the SAP CPQ product manager for this function: To display variant conditions in the Responder, the following three conditions must be met in the response payload of SAP VCP Stateless Pricing API:

  1. VarCond == true
  2. InactiveFlag in {' ','W'}
  3. Statistical = false

The first condition indicates that the pricing record is based on the variant condition key; the second one reflects the validity of the price condition record; and the third one means that the condition type is set to non-statistical type in the Pricing Procedure.

This is the current product design. If your business needs are different, you are welcomed to provide feedback in SAP CPQ (Configure Price Quote) - Customer Influence (sap.com). Your input may impact the future development of SAP's products and services. We appreciate your support and contribution.

entry.title: converting XML fields into single json string
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

My scenario : proxy <--> PI <---> rest         mode of communication : Synchronous

i have a requirement, i am getting 3 xml fields as shown in the below  in the input payload need to convert them as a single json string. could you please help on this to write an XSLT code. i am new to this xslt code.

input fields : 

client_id

client_secret

grant_type

 

Thanks in advance.

 

Best Regards,

Nagaraju

entry.content:

How can the display of name be activated?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA - Fiori: Add Apps to Business Roles which are not in a Business Catalog.

Indeed, very useful information for people starting off with S4HANA and Fiori Launchpad in it. 
Thanks a lot for sharing this info!!! 

 

Thanks Sashi

entry.title: Re: How to Construct a Deep link URL to navigate to Custo...
entry.content:
Hi Praveen,

Hope you are doing well.

I am sorry to tell you that C4C does not support this. C4C uses server-side rendering(SSR), meaning we cannot decide which part to render by deep link. (You may refer to the blog to know the basic concept of SSR Server Side Rendering in JavaScript)

You may also have been aware that the URL does not change when navigating to different tabs. This means that the URL cannot control the active tab. 

Please let me know if you have further queries on this topic.

Regards,
Alan

entry.title: Re: Can't see tickets
entry.content: Hi Rehanna, could you provide a screenshot to show case the issue here? Thanks!

Best regards,

Isyraf

entry.title: SAP BAS debugger showing unbound breakpoints
entry.content:
Hi,

When executing SAP BAS debugger, my breakpoints are unbound and are skipped but statements are being executed. Can you please suggest if I am missing something. 

launch.json:

    {
      "command": "cds serve --with-mocks --in-memory?",
      "name": "cds serve",
      "request": "launch",
      "type": "node-terminal",
      "skipFiles": [
        "<node_internals>/**"
      ]
    }

entry.title: Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correct and available -4009
entry.content: Hello expert: I use Java to connect to SBO on the server through local DI-API. It used to be normal for a while, sometimes when I tried to connect, it showed login failure to the SLD server. Last time I tried to log in successfully by deleting and then adding the SLD service, I recently encountered this issue again. After following the previous steps, I found that it does not work. Then I tried to restart and modify the SLD address in b1's local machine.xml, but found that it didn't work either. Then, I copied the SBO b1 local machine.xml file that could run normally on the server to the local server, and the same problem still exists. My SLD service has two options: server IP address and computer host name. Do you have any experience or solutions in this area? The version I am using is sbo10.0 version

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 1: How to Enable Recommendation
entry.content:
As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, you can complete sales order data with the aid of system recommendations in the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

Currently the following sales order fields support recommendation:

LevelField
HeaderSales Office
HeaderSales Group
HeaderCustomer Payment Terms
HeaderShipping Conditions
ItemPlant
ItemShipping Point

Before using the recommendation feature, complete the following steps:

 

Step 1: Activate Scope Item 73P

Note: Scope Item 73P has some region limitation. For details, please check Note 3389633 - Restrictions for Setting up Sales Order Automation (73P).

Refer to the following information to activate scope item 73P.

Type Reference Guide
Solution Builder managed (2SL)Link 1
CBC managed (2SL)Link 2
CBC managed (3SL)Link 3

To check whether 73P has been activated, you can execute prerequisite checks in the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470).

  1. Log on to your system with role SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST.

  2. Open the Intelligent Scenario Management app, and search for intelligent scenario "SLS_DOC_AUTO_COMPLTN".

  3. Click "Prerequisite Checks".

  4. Go through all steps and click "Start Prerequisite Check Run".

  5. Wait until the Overall Run Status becomes "Successful".

  6. Open check results. The third check indicates whether scope item 73P is activated in your system.

    Note: The first check indicates whether your system could connect to the Data Attribute Recommendation service. The second check indicates whether your system has enough transactional data for model training. Without enough transactional data, you still can start a training but possibly with poor quality.

 

Step 2: Activate Feature Toggle FTGL_SDACMONITOR

Solution Builder managed S/4HANA Cloud system

  1. Log on to your system with role SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT.

  2. Open the Manage Your Solution app (F1241), and click "Configure Your Solution".

  3. Search for feature toggle relevant configuration.

  4. Activate FTGL_SDACMONITOR.

CBC managed S/4HANA Cloud system

  1. Log on to your CBC system.

  2. Search for feature toggle relevant configuration.

  3. Activate FTGL_SDACMONITOR.

 

Step 3: Train Machine Learning Model

  1. Log on to your system with role SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST.

  2. Open the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470), and search for intelligent scenario "SLS_DOC_AUTO_COMPLTN".

  3. Click "Train" to start a new training.

    • Version: Choose "3.0". This field indicates the API version that you use to start a training. Currently only "3.0" is available.
    • Package Size: This field indicates the size of uploading batch. If your system doesn't have enough memory, specify a smaller number, and vice versa.

    You can customize your training data in this dialog. Please see the next blog for more details.

  4. Wait until the training status becomes "Completed".

  5. Select the training instance and click "Deploy".

  6. Wait until the deployment status becomes "Deployed".

  7. Select the deployed instance and click "Activate for Me" or "Activate for All".

Okay! Now you can use the recommendation feature in your system.

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 2: How to Customize Training
entry.content:
Training the machine learning model in the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470) is a prerequisite for using the recommendation feature. A training is based on historical transactional data in your system. In this blog, I'll show you how to start a training with your customized data.

Training Filters

In the "Training Filters" section, you can restrict training data based on particular conditions.

For example, to base your training on the data created between Jan 1 2023 to Dec 31 2023, you can set the CREATIONDATE filter.

Note: The condition option supports Between, Equal to and Not equal to.

After you start a training, you can check the filter settings and uploaded dataset size.


Training Details

In the "Training Details" section, you can specify your model description and configure target fields.

For example, if you want to enable recommendations only for field Plant, de-select other fields.

Once the training is completed, you can check training details about the target fields in the Debriefing section.

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Overview
entry.content:
The scope item 73P has been delivered as of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402. Within the scope item, you can:

  1. Complete sales order data with the aid of system recommendations in the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app

  2. Monitor recommendation details and processing in sales orders using the Monitor Recommendations for Sales Document Completion app (F7515)

Note: SAP S/4HANA Cloud consumes the machine learning capabilities of the Data Attribute Recommendation service to predict sales document data.

Features

Complete Sales Order Data Based on Recommended Values

Currently the following sales order fields support recommendation:

LevelField
HeaderSales Office
HeaderSales Group
HeaderCustomer Payment Terms
HeaderShipping Conditions
ItemPlant
ItemShipping Point

Before using this feature, ensure that certain prerequisites are met. Then you can test the feature as follows:

  1. Open the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

  2. Create a sales order with at least one item.

    Note: The machine learning model is trained on sales order item level, so your sales order must contain at least one item.

  3. Click the "Incompleteness Info" button in the sales order header.

  4. Navigate to a target incomplete field from the message popover, or click on a target field to view the recommendations.

Monitor Recommendation Details and Processing in Sales Orders

You can view all sales orders with recommendations in the Monitor Recommendations for Sales Document Completion app (F7515).

List Page

You can choose the "Complete Document Data" button to navigate to the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

When you click on a hyperlink in the "With Recommendations" column, recommendation details are displayed in a popover:

You can choose a "Log" icon to display the completion log of the sales order:

A sales order field with recommendations can have the following statuses:

StatusCase
Acceptance PendingThis field has recommendations, and it is empty now.
Recommendation AcceptedOne of the recommended values has been manually selected.
Entered ManuallyA value other than the recommended values has been manually entered.

 

Blog Series Navigation

Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 1: How to Enable Recommendation

Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 2: How to Customize Training

entry.title: How can I add a new communication scenario to my SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi experts, I'm trying to add a new communication scenario to the 'Communication Arrangement' app in my SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Specifically, I'm trying to use the module SAP_COM_0120 related to the billing document, but it does not appear on the 'Create' page. Could you guide me on how to add this module typically? I've reviewed some documents and might need to adjust them from SAP Netweaver. Could you confirm if this is the correct procedure? Your assistance in this matter is highly valued and appreciated.

 

Here's the capture on my list.

I would like to know if it is related to Odata V2 (SOAP API) so I can't add it or if I don't have the privileges to establish this module with Communication Arrangement.

 

Attached is the API file description I need.

Overview | Billing Document - Read, Cancel, GetPDF | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

entry.title: Re: Latest Updates to the Reimagined Applicant Management Tool in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

Can you please clarify what does it mean by Admin Universal Push? 2H 2024 Release (Nov): Admin Universal Push and End of Support for the Legacy Workbench.

Is my understanding correct?

In 2H 2024 release, the redesigned applicant workbench will be enabled for all, and we cannot enable or disable this in recruiting settings. Also, we cannot toggle between legacy and redesigned applicant workbench. we will only have the redesigned applicant workbench?

Please clarify.

Thank you!

Ankita

https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap-ux/backend-proxy-middleware

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot Not Generated despite the correct Mast...
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

Let me know check this with security team.

entry.title: Re: SAP BAS debugger showing unbound breakpoints
entry.content: The scripts in UI run in the browser. You need to set breakpoints in the browser (developer tools).

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot Not Generated despite the correct Mast...
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

Thanks a lot for reply.

Please see response inline.

"inspection lots are generating sometimes and on other occasions it is not" depends what the "other occasions' are.

If another material -> Is this material active with the inspection type in the material? ---- It is the same material, inspection type is active. 

If inbound delivery is used -> inspection type with HU activated in the material master? ---- Inbound delivery is used, inspection type with HU is not  activated in the material master.

If another plant -> Is this plant also active for inspection? ---- Plant is same.

If the same material & purchase order -> Is the material setting 'Control InspLot' that each GR has it own inspection lot (and not one inspection lot per purchase order item, one inspection lot per material)? ---- Setting for 'Control InspLot' (Blank) For each material document item.

Hope that this inspires you to find the cause, but I assume it is master data related. ---- Currently ABAPer is debugging as per SAP KBA 3017532 inspection lot is influenced by customer exit.

entry.title: Given a date, how to find the nearest Monday using SAC Data Action
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Given a date in integer format, how to find the nearest Monday of it in SAP SAC data action. 

We have a date in interger format in a measure called: "Shipment Date", that will be entered by the planner, and we need to find the nearest Monday after 10 days, that's the max "Delivery by Date".

So, for example, Shipment date is: 05/27/2024,  the delivery date should be calculated as: 06/03/2024, instead of 06/10/2024, as this is the nearest Monday to date: 06/06/2024, when added 10 days to 05/27/2024. 

Thanks,

SP

entry.title: Re: Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correc...
entry.content:
Hi ZHD 

Please verify SLD address, for the file "b1-local-machine.xml"  from : C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAP Business One DI API\Conf

If still having issue, you could try to reinstall DI API.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: use odata in sapui5 (Invalid Function Import Parameter )
entry.content:
Hello,

If you’re trying to access Function Import then it should be oModel.callFunction

Best Regards,

Viswanath

entry.title: MDK Custom Client 24.4.1 + iOS TestFlight Upload Failing - ITMS-90338: Non-public API usage
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are preparing our app for Apple App store distribution through XCode and to give it to our testers through TestFlight.

We have created a custom MDK Client 24.4.1 and use our certificates and provisioning profiles to package the app.

The builds are not available in TestFlight and an email is recieved stating:

Hello,

We noticed one or more issues with a recent delivery for the following app:

  • App Name
  • Version 1.0.0
  • Build 8

Please correct the following issues and upload a new binary to App Store Connect.

ITMS-90338: Non-public API usage - The app references non-public selectors in Frameworks/SAPMDC.framework/SAPMDC: setNumberOfRowsInSection:. If method names in your source code match the private Apple APIs listed above, altering your method names will help prevent this app from being flagged in future submissions. In addition, note that one or more of the above APIs may be located in a static library that was included with your app. If so, they must be removed. For further information, visit the Technical Support Information at http://developer.apple.com/support/technical/

Apple Developer Relations

 

Can you please assist?

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.11
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.11 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.407 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP
  • Data Exploration
    • Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer
    • Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer
  • Data Integration
    • Data Export API - Delta Difference
    • Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections
  • Story Design
    • Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides
    • Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations
  • Story Extensibility
    • Retrieving non-input-ready variables as dynamic text or through script API

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP

If you accidentally delete your service instance in the SAP BTP cockpit, it can be restored within seven days. Learn more here.

 

Data Exploration

 

Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer

A "+Add Calculations" button has been added in the Structure section of the Available Objects panel. This allows the creation of calculations using structure members in two structured queries on BW.

 

Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer

The last used values for defined components and interactions are now kept so that when data analyzer is reopened, the options are the same. That way as a data analyzer user you can now set the following:

  • Show/hide the filter line by default
  • Open/close individually the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list 
  • Expand/collapse the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list by default
  • Set the system and connection type by default in the data source browser
  • Keep all settings in the individual export dialogs for PDF, Microsoft Excel and CSV export (except for Export name)

 

Data Integration

 

Data Export API - Delta Difference

Data Export Service API subscriptions has been extended to support the calculation of the difference between old and new numerical delta values when performing a Delta Extraction, rather than just new measure values.

 

Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections

Users can now select Client Certificate authentication and use an X.509 certificate for generic OData connections, SAP Business ByDesign Analytics and SAP S4/HANA Cloud connections.

 

Story Design

 

Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides

The optimized story experience now supports export as Google Slides. Google Slides can be saved as a new presentation, or the slides can be added to an existing presentation in a Google Drive folder.

This is not supported in the lite viewer.

 

Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations

The Lite Viewer now supports color by dimension for the bubble layer in geo maps. Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension will no longer cause an automatic redirect to the Full Viewer.

There are few improvements that have been made to the interactivity of Geographical Visualizations which includes:

  • Improved Interaction with Color by Dimension Bubble
  • Consolidation of Drill with Tooltip
  • Improved Threshold Tooltip

 

Story Extensibility

In the optimized story experience, dynamic text and variable related script APIs such as getVariables and getVariableValues now support non-input-ready variables.

Retrieve the variables that are not input ready, such as customer exit variables, from a live connection to SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW):

  • As Dynamic Text
  • Through script APIs
    • getDataSource().getVariableValues()
    • getDataSource().getVariables

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Purchase requisition created in discontinued material...
entry.content: Greetings,

entry.title: Re: How to mark the top of the SAC filter panel
entry.content:
Hi heejujoung, 

  From your screenshot, seems you are in the new 'Present mode' of the story. I would suggest:

  • Still open in view mode by default. You can set it via story setting -> View time setting -> Navigation -> open default in view mode. 
  • Not showing filter panel by default in view mode. You can set it via story setting -> View time setting  -> Filter panel -> View time configuration -> Hidden .

Best regards, William

entry.title: Brands created in CSB is not appearing in Manage Data > MarketingBrand
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have created brands in CSB, however this is not reflecting in Manage Data > MarketingBrand. 

My user has permissions to object MarketingBrand. I have complete Odata refresh as suggested in one of the KBAs. 

Is there anything that I need to complete ? Any help on this will be great. 

Just additional information: This was configured in Development instance previously and it was working fine. 

Now while configuring in Test instance, I am not seeing the brands in Manage Data. This in turn blocks me from creating a business rule. 

Note: Unified Data model is not being used.

entry.title: Re: Purchase requisition created in discontinued material...
entry.content:
Greetings,

you can try setting MRP type to ND at material master MRP 1 tab.

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: Pricing based on custom attributes with SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content: That's very informative. Thanks for sharing. Is there any blog available to understand how to do mathematical calculations. basically, I want to take the price output from look up table and multiply with another parameter to get final amount. How to achieve this?

entry.title: Clean Core Approach for SAP Fiori Development
entry.content:
I am still a bit confused with clean core concept with regards with Fiori/UI5 development. I hope someone can enlighten me. 

For example we have a business requirement to extend/enhance a standard app.

There are a few ways on how to do this

1. Key User Adaptation. If the requirement can be satisfied via AdaptUI or creation of custom fields / logic using Standard Fiori Apps Custom Fields and Custom Logic. This I know is clearly clean core approach.

2. Component Configuration. We still do use this kind of extension specially for the old Fiori applications. 

Is using extension hooks considered as clean core? How about view replacement? controller extensions? controller replacements? Are these considered clean core approach? 

3. Controller Extension via Adaptation project. I believe this is also clean core approach right?

4. Creation of a z copy of the standard app and deploying it as a new app. App will either use a standard OData service, a custom OData service or a z copy of a standard OData service. Is this way clean core also?

entry.title: Re: Adobe forms - & character
entry.content:
If you are maintaining the text as Header Text, click on the 'Detail' icon at the bottom to launch the Text Editor. Re-write the sentence with &. This will appear as '&' in Adobe PDF.

 

Hope that helps!

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇷 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (São Paulo, Brazi
entry.content: 👏 see you at the event!

entry.title: SAUG BTP Hackathon
entry.content:
During the build phase, the SAP team will assist and hand hold the teams to help them in the implementation of their SAP BTP use cases. SAUG and SAP will help you to work with SAP BTP and also help you build a working prototype for your business use cases. SAP’s team of global BTP experts will enable participating teams on the nuances of the implementation / usage of SAP BTP and will share SAP’s experience and knowledge. There will be 6 enablement webinars which will be deep dives of various BTP technologies and applications. 
 
 
Finalists will be invited to show off their projects in a Demo Jam at the SAUG National Summit in August. The winners will receive recognition and rewards and the whole community will benefit with outstanding learnings from real use cases.
 
We encourage members to send their submissions, nominate their teams and take part in the valuable knowledge building. This will be a great learning opportunity which will develop real-life BTP use cases and deliver direct business value to your organisation.

To know more details of the Hackathon and how to join, please refer to this webpage on SAUG Website

https://www.saug.com.au/Communities/SAUG_BTP_Hackathon

entry.title: Re: Multiple Profit center issue in Delta accrual document
entry.content:
Hello Pankaj,

Before implementing any custom logic try this configuration:

Under Settlement Documents -> Settlement Document, find your delta accrual settlement document type and under the "Accounting" tab, change the setting "Partner Lines at Item Level" to "Yes". Then try to reprocess the delta accrual settlement.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Instead of customer account, Expense GL should hit fo...
entry.content:
Hi @trinath80 

I saw your post which says you want the accounting entries to be shown as:

Customer A/c Dr

Revenue A/c Dr

VAT Expense A/c Dr

         To Customer A/c Cr

         To Revenue A/c Cr

         To VAT Payable A/c Cr

If there a legal requirement for such a flow? Why would you show a FOC Sale to revenue?

Ideally, for FOC sale to calculate the tax, you would move tax condition after VPRS and than do a new condition type with 100% discount after the tax condition in your pricing condition. You could make the tax condition type as accrual so that it posts the tax as you want.

The below question answered in the past, might be of help for FOC Related pricing.

Solved: Free of charge item tax calculation - SAP Community

Thanks & Regards

Sanil Bhandari

Thank you, best regards.

Pavel Paulus

entry.title: Re: How to use OpenTelemetry for SAP Cloud Foundry / Clou...
entry.content:
Hello BarcDe,

I am doing a Proof of Concept to explore OpenTelemetry of SAP Cloud ALM for BTP Cloud Foundry environment, for example, to monitor custom application deployed on it.

I reached to following SAP Documentation but I did not understand what exactly is to be done. Perhaps you could find it useful:

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/data-collection-infrastructure.html

I have another question open here on community.sap.com.

From your question, I realised some other steps which were not documented, at least I did not find it.

Would it be possible for you to share stepwise procedure which you described?

  1. Inserted the destination.
  2. Created a service key in the CALM subaccount.
  3. Set up the Alert Notification Service in the CF subaccount.
  4. Added the Cloud Foundry subaccount to the scope selection.
  5. Activated monitoring.

Warm regards,

Amey

Any tips, please.

Regards,

MI

entry.title: Re: Time Off Recruit Rule: Rounding up in 0.5s
entry.content:
Hi Sorai,

I did in fact find that previously but I was looking to round up as to down, and struggled anyway to replicate it.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 4
entry.content: Sure, let me try again.

entry.title: Re: Date shown wrong in SAP Analytics Cloud based on SAP...
entry.content:
Hello @tilted 

this is a well-known missing feature (for German Datasphere users) that SAP does not provide an in-built function to display a German date format 😪

What you see here is the internal key for the dates, like stored on the database.

We (German users) have to create our own date format in an additional (text) view... which you associate to your date field... then in the SAC story you can display the text as dd.mm.yyyy, and the key is still the internal format.

Formula: CONCAT(Tag,CONCAT('.',CONCAT(MONTH,CONCAT('.',YEAR))))

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the SAP Community Katowice

Pozdrawiam,

Krzysiek

entry.title: Re: How to use OpenTelemetry for SAP Cloud Foundry / Clou...
entry.content:
Hello BarcDe,

I am doing a Proof of Concept to explore OpenTelemetry of SAP Cloud ALM for BTP Cloud Foundry environment, for example, to monitor custom application deployed on it.

I reached to the same SAP Documentation and facing same issue as you. Do not understand what exactly is to be done. I have a custom sample CAPM based application deployed on my Cloud Foundry Subaccount, but this steps mentioned here are too vague to follow.

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/data-collection-infrastructure.html

I have another question open here on community.sap.com:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/doing-a-proof-of-concept-for-btp-application-monitoring-with-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/13703181#M167525

From your question, I realised some other steps which were not documented, at least I did not find it.

Would it be possible for you to share stepwise procedure which you described?

  1. Inserted the destination.
  2. Created a service key in the CALM subaccount.
  3. Set up the Alert Notification Service in the CF subaccount.
  4. Added the Cloud Foundry subaccount to the scope selection.
  5. Activated monitoring.

Warm regards,

Amey

And of course i use the dimension table in my facts table. any suggestions?

Finally, succussed 🙂.  

Here is the screenshot @noravonthenen .

entry.title: Google’s Third-Party Cookie Depreciation in the Kyma Environment
entry.content:
Third-party cookies are set by domains other than the one a user is directly visiting. They are commonly used for online advertising and tracking user behavior across multiple websites. They can be set through JavaScript, iframe, embedded images, or media. SAP primarily uses third-party cookies for service management and single sign-on.
 
Google has announced its plan to phase out support for third-party cookies by the third quarter of 2024. See The Privacy Sandbox Timeline for the Web. However, you can use the third-party deprecation trial, which allows you to re-enable third-party cookies for a page embedded within a cross-site iframe until December 27, 2024.
 

Apply Third-Party Cookie Deprecation Trial Tokens for SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime Workloads

 
To enable the depreciation trial, all applications’ responses must include a deprecation trial token obtained for each affected domain. At SAP, the process of obtaining the tokens is handled centrally. There are three different ways to add the deprecation trial token - you can either provide it in an HTTP header, use a meta tag, or inject the token with JavaScript. For the SAP BTP, Kyma runtime workloads, it is recommended that you use the HTTP header. You must add the token manually for each of the affected workloads. To learn how to do this, read Deprecation Trial for Google's Third-Party Cookies and follow the tutorial Apply Third-Party Cookie Deprecation Trial Tokens for Kyma Runtime Workloads.
 

Related Links

  • Preparing and Testing Your Solution for Third-Party Cookie Deprecation
  • SAP Note 3409306 - Removal of Third-Party Cookies in Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge Browser

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 4
entry.content:
Hi @gphadnis2000 ,

Please set trainingExecutableIdRegression = 340abda5-d605-452a-bf4c-169249aab362 in step 2 and then follow the instructions. You will have the results in step 6.

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Clarifica...
entry.content: @stefan_kiene Does this mean activation of scope id (4GT - Solution Order Management) is not mandatory to use Subscription Billing?

entry.title: Re: How to 'deploy' roles when using IAS for a CAP applic...
entry.content:
IMO there's no way around `xsuaa` as it is the mapper between Auth{X,Z} source and its application at runtime. 

In other words: no matter what single source of truth you use, whether it is the standard SAP Cloud IdP, or a custom IdP (in the subaccount) or IAS, `xsuaa` is always required to map authentication and authorisation into the CAP runtime.

SOLMAN 7.2 SP15

entry.title: Replenishment based Supplier Managed Inventory with SAP Business Network
entry.content:
Supplier Managed Inventory (SMI also known as VMI) is a supply chain initiative where the supplier takes responsibility for managing inventory levels and replenishment for their customers. Replenishment-based VMI focuses specifically on ensuring that inventory levels are maintained to meet customer demand while minimizing excess stock and shortages.

Key Components

  1. Data Sharing:
  • Sales Data: Customers share sales data with suppliers to provide insights into demand patterns.
  • Inventory Levels: Real-time inventory levels are communicated to the supplier.
  • Forecasts: Customers may share their demand forecasts, which suppliers use to plan production and distribution.
  1. Automated Replenishment:
  • Suppliers use inventory data to trigger replenishment orders automatically when stock levels fall below predefined thresholds.
  • Algorithms and software systems can optimize order quantities and timing to balance supply with demand.
  1. Agreements and Contracts:
  • Service level agreements (SLAs) outline the performance metrics, such as fill rates, delivery times, and inventory levels.
  • Contracts define the responsibilities, processes, and financial terms between the supplier and customer.
  1. Technology Integration:  
  • Advanced IT systems and ERP software facilitate seamless data exchange and processing.

Benefits for Buyer

  1. Improved Inventory Management:
  • Reduces stockouts and excess inventory, leading to lower holding costs and improved cash flow.
  • Enhances inventory accuracy and visibility across the supply chain.
  1. Cost Savings:
  • Reduces administrative costs associated with placing and managing orders.
  • Economies of scale in transportation and order processing can be achieved.
  • Improve the working capital with optimized inventory.
  1. Increased Service Levels:
  • Higher fill rates and improved delivery performance enhance customer satisfaction.
  • Responsive and flexible supply chain capable of adapting to demand fluctuations.
  • Improve service levels by having the product at the right time and location.
  1. Efficiency Gains:
  • Streamlined replenishment processes reduce lead times and improve supply chain responsiveness.
  • Better alignment of supply and demand reduces waste and inefficiencies.
  • Reduce planning and ordering costs by shifting the responsibility to the supplier.

Benefits for Supplier

  • Visibility of the buyer’s demand and inventory data enables more accurate forecasting and planning.
  • Reduce buyer ordering errors resulting in a decrease in potential returns.
  • Visibility of stock levels enables proper prioritization of orders.
  • Stabilizing the timing of order generation based on a predefined basis improves supply reliability.
  • A true partnership is formed between the buyer and the supplier. They work closer together and strengthen their ties.

Replenishment based Supplier Managed Inventory Collaboration with SAP Business Network

SMI – Supplier Managed Inventory provides foundational capability for Supply Chain Collaboration between Buyers and Suppliers by sharing inventory, demand information and replenishment of inventory based on established Min/Max levels.

There are two variation of Supplier Managed Inventory Collaboration process as shown below

SMI Process Flow (Planned Shipment) with Scheduling Agreement

SMI Process Flow (Replenishment Order)

SMI Process Flow (Planned Shipment) with Scheduling Agreement process explanation

Step 1 --> The Material master is configured for SMI process in the MRP Area as shown below.

Step 2 -->Creation of Scheduling Agreement Release with pricing details.

Step 3 -->Extract SMI relevant data by executing T-code ARBCIG_PROA_SMI_EXTRACT in SAP S4HANA and send them to SAP Business Network.

Step 4 --> SMI key figure configurations and formula. Custom key figures can be added and associated formula can configure as necessary.

Step 5 --> Supplier View of SMI Relevant data and Key Figures including (Stock on hand , Min / Max, projected stock , Lead times , gross demand , stock out situations etc.

Supplier can get the insights with respect to Inventory situations over the time series in a graphical manner.

 

SAP Business network can calculate using formulas and provide insights with respect to Projected stock situations , Days of Inventory etc.

Based on information available and supplier side situation, supplier can create planned shipment from SAP Business Network as shown below.

Step 5 --> Once the supplier submit the Planned Shipment, with integration the Planned shipment quantity would be added as Delivery schedule line of the Scheduling Agreement. The rest of the process of ASN and GR and Invoice would continue as per the normal scenarios.

Key figure Descriptions

  • ASN Received:  The total Goods Received quantity is an AN Calculated based on the ASN that was received against the ASN Delivery Date referencing the time bucket.
  • Firmed Orders: The total Purchase Order or Scheduling Agreement quantity is an AN Calculated where the delivery date matches the time bucket.
  • Gross Demand: The total gross requirements in the buyer’s system that was transferred to AN via cXML element Gross Demand.
  • In-Transit: The In-transit quantity is an AN Calculated value based on the ASN created by Supplier where the delivery date matches the time bucket. The In-transit will not include ASN’s that are Goods Receipted.
  • Maximum Proposal: The Maximum Proposal is AN Calculated formula driven Key figure where Maximum Proposal Stock is the quantity needed to attain the Maximum Stock level.                      The formula that is represented is "Projected Stock – Max Stock"
  • Minimum Proposal: The Minimum Proposal is AN Calculated formula driven Key figure where Minimum Proposal Stock is the quantity needed to attain the Minimum Stock level.                        The formula that is represented is  "Projected Stock – Minimum Stock".
  • Net Requirement: The Total Net Requirement in the buyers system that was transferred to AN via cXML element Net Requirement.
  • Order Received:  The total Order Received quantity is an AN Calculated based on the Goods Received against the PO in the AN based on the PO delivery Date.
  • Planned Shipment : The Planned Shipment is the Supplier entered value based on Supplier production schedule.
  • Target Stock : The Total Target Stock maintained in the buyers system that was transferred to AN via cXML element Target Stock.
  • Total Shipment : AN Calculated Key figure that represents the total Shipment(In-transit or Received) for each time period based on the Delivery Date of the ASN.
  • Projected Stock :  The projected stock is a key figure that is critical for Inventory visibility. AN calculates the projected stock from demands, receipts, and stocks. The projected stock provides information about the demand/stock balance of a location product in a period. The projected stock is the stock of a product that is expected to be available at the location at the end of a period. A Buyer or Supplier can ascertain from this key figure how the demand/stock balance develops over time and whether critical stock situations occur.
  • Current time period projected stock : Today’s projected stock = Stock + all receipts with delivery date today – all demands with demand date today.
  • Projected stock on any later day : Projected stock of this day = projected stock of the previous day + receipts with delivery date on this day − demands with demand date on this day.

Conclusion

The success of a replenishment based VMI system depends on effective collaboration, accurate data sharing, robust technology, clear agreements, and continuous improvement. By focusing on these critical factors, suppliers and customers can create a mutually beneficial VMI system that enhances inventory management, reduces costs, and improves overall supply chain performance.

Also when we select any record from the table and then click on Create, it is calling the CreatewithorderType after create it is giving error "Resource not found for segment 'com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.CreateWithSalesOrderType'"

Sarita

Document splitting is activated.

We are getting Exchange gain & Exchange loss both in the same document. Is this also due to the post per line item activation.

If not, why it is like that. Please confirm.

S/4 HANA 2022 version

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Kasigari

entry.title: Re: Datasphere- Unique constrain violation in G/L Hierarc...
entry.content:
Hello @sanjoypoddar10 

It is possible that your target table is missing the key setting in the "Dateto" field?

In this way it might be possible that you cause "duplicate keys" when inserting data into it.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Gain insights on processes having complex relationship using SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
The Process Visibility Capability of SAP Build Process Automation offers an end to end view of heterogenous processes . Process fragments running on different systems have to be correlated to get an end to end view and the following forms of correlation are supported. 

  • 1: 1 Correlation
    • This form of correlation is defined when an instance of one process is associated to exactly one instance of another process. For example, consider an employee onboarding process in SAP SuccessFactors that is triggered for a new hire. This process triggers an extension workflow on SAP BTP for equipment provisioning . A 1:1 correlation exists between the processes as each new hire onboarding process triggers an equipment procurement process.
  • N:1 Correlation
    • This form of correlation is defined when multiple instances of a process relate to exactly one instance of another process. For example, consider a scenario having an order and a delivery processes where multiple orders are part of the same delivery. There exists an N:1 correlation between the processes as multiple orders can be associated with a single delivery.
  • 1: N Correlation
    • This form of correlation is defined when one instance of the primary process is associated with multiple instances of the secondary process. For example, consider Capital Expenditure Approval  process that initiates multiple instances of approval process dynamically based on the number of mandatory approvers. In such cases, it is important to track the primary process instances and also monitor the associated secondary process instances that it starts.

In this blog , I will take you through the steps to configure a dasboard that enables business users to track end to end processes having 1:N relation. Let's look at the example of a Capital Expenditure Approval process. As soon as a request  is submitted, a process is triggered. This request needs multiple approvals where the Manager, Cost Center Owner and Vice President need to review & approve the request. This is an example where the primary process (Capital Expenditure Process) starts multiple secondary process instances (Approval process).

 Process Owners overseeing the Capital Expenditure process require visibility into the present status of requests and the ability to monitor associated approval requests.

To configure one such dashboard , follow the steps given below :

Prerequisites:

  • You have subscribed to SAP Build Process Automation . For details, refer documentation
  • You have two processes that have a 1:N relationship & these processes could be of type SPA Process, BTP Workflow ,Observed Process or Business Workflow.

Procedure

  1. Create a primary visibility scenario in your project and add a process as a participant to your visibility scenario. In this example, Capital Expenditure Approval is the primary process added to the scenario. Refer to the documentation to create a visibility scenario.
  2. Create a second visibility scenario in the same project and add a process as a participant.In this example,  Approval process is added to the scenario. Ensure that you have atleast one common context attribute (defined with the same path & attribute ID in both scenarios)  to link the instances across the primary and secondary scenario. In this example, Request ID is the common context attribute as shown below.

     

  3. To enable drill down of instances from primary scenario to the secondary, we need to do the following :
    • In the primary scenario, create a calculated attribute of the expression type Constant and datatype String and enter the desired value for the attribute  eg (View Approvals)
    • Configure a navigation link for the above attribute as given below. 
      • Example:<Site URL>/site#pv-showInstances?sap-app-origin-hint=&sap-ushell-navmode=inplace&scenarioId=<scenarioID>&/filter=<context attribute ID> eq '<Value>'
      • Site URL - This is the site URL that is configured using SAP Build Workzone standard edition.
      • scenarioID - Scenario ID of the secondary scenario. You can find the scenario ID in Visibility Scenarios application in Monitoring page
      • Context Attribute ID - This is the common context attribute ID and the value can be passed dynamically while configuring the link as shown below. The dynamic value will be resolved when the link is clicked
  4. Save your project.
  5. Release and Deploy your project. 
  6. You have started instances for both the processes or alternatively pushed events as documented here.
  7. From the Monitoring page, click on the Visibility Scenarios tile and check if the instances are processed for both the scenarios.
  8. Configure Process Workspace application in SAP Build Workzone as outlined in the documentation.
  9. Open the Visibility Scenario dashboard for the primary scenario & navigate to its scenario instances.
  10. Click on the link View Approvals to navigate to the instances of the secondary scenario.

    With this procedure, you can achieve any level of navigation from one scenario to another to effectively track instances and monitor their progress.

entry.title: Margin Analysis
entry.content:
Dear former members,

When I'm trying to execute Top-down distribution cycle in Margin analysis getting this error

"Sender and reference data is not mapping".

Senders data is getting correctly when executing cycle but there are no receivers.

I posted an fright expense to a profitability segment( Material group) which is reflecting in KE24 Report.

Posted sales with the 2 materials in the same material group, there are only 2 materials in that material group.

TD Template is not properly configured in my view but don't know how to correct it.

Please guide me in resolving this.

Fiori S/4 Hana 2022 version.

Points will be assigned accordingly.

Thanks in advance

Regards,

Kasigari

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Web Assistant is covering the application...
entry.content: Hi Anna

entry.title: Re: Disable search option in Select Dialog
entry.content:
Hi ,

We Can hide/remove the search bar from SelectDialog using _searchField method.I am providing the snippet for your understanding.

 var oView = this.getView();
                if (!this._pDialog) {
                    this._pDialog = this.loadFragment({
                        name: "path",
                        controller: this
                    }).then(function (oDialog) {
                        oDialog._searchField.setVisible(false);
                        return oDialog;
                    });
                }

                this._pDialog.then(function (oDialog) {
                    oDialog._searchField.setVisible(false);
                    oDialog.open();
                }.bind(this));
 
SAPUI5

entry.title: SAP eDocument installation
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We're eager to integrate the SAP E-Invoicing solution into our company's S4HANA On-premise system. As newcomers to this field, we're seeking guidance on accessing the solution. Do we need to acquire the SAP DRC solution alongside the E-Invoicing solution? What are the prerequisites for implementation, and how can we proceed with the installation process? Your assistance in navigating these queries would be greatly appreciated.

@erika_buson 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance #

entry.title: Need maximum permissible limit to configure number of amendment phases in contract template
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have already configured Contract workspace template.

This contains 10 amendment phases, Amendment phases were added by referring support document 'How do I set up amendment tasks' (method3). All steps were followed as given in the document.

In many contracts, these 10 amendment phases are already used or exhausted. We want to add maximum amendment phases to avoid this situation in near future.

Requirement:-

1 'How many maximum amendment phases?' we can configure. What is the maximum permissible limit to configure amendment phases? This limit is not mentioned in the support document. 

2. If we can not configure the amendment phases beyond certain limit, can we configure Approval task with 'Repeat for each draft' for some volatile nature of commodities? This Approval task will be parallel/additional to earlier arrangement  for certain commodities.

Please provide your valuable response.

entry.title: Re: Supply Protection and Segmentation
entry.content: With OP2023 FPS2 Segments are also supported as Planning Level. This is an interesting feature, if e.g. different quality-grades are protected.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – ZIP and Compress Capabilities: Encoder, Decoder, Splitter, Aggregate
entry.content:
Hello Tarun

I would suggest to use tracing to understand where the file/payload gets lost. Is it in the gather step? Maybe you use a dedicated file name and not *.xml for testing? 

If you do not find the problem, I would suggest you open a ticket on LOD-HCI-PI-CON-SOAP so that the experts could have a look whats going wrong. Please share the solution afterwards.

Thank you

Mandy

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi,

Has anyone create an outbound delvery from the Inhouse repair.

I am trying with the standard Action Profile REPA2002 and with Action defination Create_outbound_Delivery2 with Method REPA_RETURN_to_customer. But somehow this action is not triggered.

Does anyone has any idea as to how to create an outbound delivery from Inhouse repair?

Regards

Sambit
